assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Detailed findings (lines 66-76) list all 5 criteria as PASS with code-level evidence. All criterion-N.md files (1 through 5) each show 'Verdict: PASS' covering: single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 PR files match task specification exactly (0 out-of-scope, 0 unimplemented)'. Detailed findings (lines 29-35) list all three files: 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modify)', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modify)', 'tests/api/package.rs (create)' with 'Out-of-scope files: none' and 'Unimplemented files: none'.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR'. Report line 109: 'N/A -- No review comments exist on this PR. No sub-tasks created.' No subtask-N.md files exist in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are verification/reporting artifacts. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report presents 'Overall: PASS' (line 17) with 'All checks pass. The PR implements the license filter feature as specified in the task description.' (line 19). No merge action, gh pr merge command, or auto-merge directive appears anywhere in the report. Findings are presented for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Detailed findings (lines 53-55): 'All added lines across the three changed files were scanned against the full pattern catalog (hardcoded passwords, API keys/tokens, private keys, .env files, cloud credentials, database connection strings)... No sensitive material was found.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file with 4 test functions)'. Detailed findings (lines 101-103): 'The only test file in the PR is tests/api/package.rs, which is a newly created file (80 lines added, 4 new test functions). No existing test files were modified or deleted. All test changes are purely additive.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: Report organizes detailed findings under four domain headers: 'From Intent Alignment' (lines 25-49) with Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability; 'From Security' (lines 51-55) with Sensitive Pattern Scan; 'From Correctness' (lines 57-81) with CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands; 'From Style/Conventions' (lines 83-103) with Convention Upgrade, Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, Test Change Classification. Summary table (lines 5-15) includes all checks. Test Quality row (line 13) explicitly combines: 'Repetitive Test Detection: PASS; Test Documentation: PASS; Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 29-35): file-by-file listing with modify/create annotations and out-of-scope/unimplemented counts. Security (lines 53-55): describes scanning 'All added lines across the three changed files' against 'full pattern catalog' listing specific pattern types. Correctness (lines 66-76): per-criterion code-level evidence citing function names, SQL patterns, and test names. Style/Conventions (lines 89-103): individual assessment of 4 test functions for repetitiveness and documentation format. These go well beyond the summary table.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS; Test Documentation: PASS; Eval Quality: N/A'. Detailed findings (lines 97-99): 'Eval Quality -- N/A. No eval result reviews found in the PR. No reviews match the 3-criteria eval result detection heuristic (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals).' Test Quality is PASS (not affected by N/A Eval Quality).

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: No subtask-N.md files exist in the outputs directory (only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). Report confirms no sub-tasks were created (line 109: 'No sub-tasks created'). Eval Quality is N/A (line 98), so no eval failure sub-tasks are warranted.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: Both verdicts appear in the summary table independently of domain sub-agent sections: Review Feedback (line 5): 'N/A | No review comments exist on this PR'; Root-Cause Investigation (line 6): 'N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. They also have independent sections at the bottom of the report (lines 108-113), separate from the four 'From [Domain]' detailed findings sections.
